what_it_does: A build specification for a dynamic, registry-driven pilot tool-face UI that loads tools from a live FastMCP registry, renders them through resolver archetypes, integrates chain and graph execution on local models, and frames the interface as an open-world navigable space.
observations: Build spec for PILOT mode tool-face; explicitly not handcrafted or static.
Four mapped requirements: dynamic tool loading, open-world game-design interface, chains+graph execution, and single-place dynamic reflection.
Current constraints: chrome-devtools is down and /api/tools is 404 until reboot, so this window is research/design/spec plus code that does not need live verification.

Grounding claims from old-UI mining: graph make→wire→configure→run→read loop already exists and is registry-driven; cascade/chain UI does not exist; local-model runs already work; two browser channels (dedicated /api/* routes and generic /api/tools/invoke).
Single real backend gap identified: save_cascade/run_cascade lack dedicated routes and are not yet on the generic invoke door.
Anchor/anti-fan-out caution: decision-card archetype keystone must land first; prove-on-one before scaling.
Sequencing is mostly post-reboot, with design/spec work now and live verification after reboot.
Defined lanes: lead, DNA, composition, projection, fork.
standouts: Tool list resolves live from /api/tools joined with form_meta; never a hardcoded list.
Interface conceived as a navigable open-world game space (radial map, camera perspectives, RHM/V companion).
Old graph loop endpoints are to be reused wholesale; only the face is rebuilt.
Chain/cascade builder face is net-new because no old UI existed for it.
save_cascade/run_cascade route gap is the one backend build needed for chains.
conventions: Uses wiki-style/cross-file bracket links and explicit file references.
Uses lanes, sequencing, and verify sections typical of build specs.
Uses star bullets, uppercase emphasis, and ★ markers for key points.
Mixes high-level design vision, engineering constraints, backend gaps, and verification criteria in one document.
concerns: Very broad scope (dynamic loading, open-world design, chains, graphs, local models) despite anti-fan-out cautions.
Most verification is blocked until reboot, so much of the file is currently unverifiable.
Relies on old-UI mining findings being accurate and endpoints remaining stable post-reboot.
The backend cascade route gap could block the chains feature if not resolved.
notes: A post-reboot build spec for a registry-driven, open-world pilot tool interface that reuses old graph endpoints and adds a new chain/cascade face, with a keystone dependency on the decision-card archetype.
questions: What is the exact schema and file location of form_meta/tool_meta.json?
What are the precise old-UI endpoint paths beyond the examples given?
What do 'RHM/V companion' and the 'perspectives' resolver axis mean concretely?
How exactly does the tool-card archetype reuse the existing decision-card renderArchetype engine?
What distinguishes 'pilot mode' from 'standard mode' in concrete UI/behavior terms?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a design/build spec with research grounding, not executable code.
apparent_themes: Reuse backend wiring, rebuild the face.
intention_signals: To create a next-generation operator interface where the company’s tools, chains, and graphs are surfaced automatically as an explorable world, runnable on local models, with minimal hardcoded UI and maximal reuse of existing bridge endpoints.
novelty: Treats the pilot tool interface as an open-world game environment with camera perspectives and a companion guide, while making every tool, chain, and graph type resolve dynamically from live registries.
contribution: A concrete integration plan mapping dynamic tool registry loading, chain/graph execution loops, and open-world UI concepts onto specific backend endpoints, archetypes, and build lanes; it also flags the keystone dependency and the single backend gap.
summary_for_embedding: PILOT-TOOLFACE-BUILD.md is a build specification for a dynamic, registry-driven pilot tool interface that renders tools through resolver archetypes, reuses existing graph execution endpoints, adds a chain/cascade face, and frames interaction as an open-world navigable space with local-model execution.
intention_for_embedding: To plan the post-reboot construction and verification of a single-place-dynamic, open-world pilot tool-face for operating the company’s tools, chains, and graphs on local models.
